---
title: "gamma_contrast"
generated: true
description: "Apply a gamma tone curve within a normalised intensity bracket."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.augment.intensity`](../nitrix-augment-intensity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gamma_contrast -->
```python
gamma_contrast(x: Float[Array, '...'], gamma: Float[Array, '...'], *, value_range: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gamma_contrast -->
Apply a gamma tone curve within a normalised intensity bracket.

Normalises `x` to `[0, 1]` over the bracket, raises it to
`gamma`, then maps back to the bracket:
    normed = clip((x - lo) / (hi - lo), 0, 1)
    out    = normed ** gamma * (hi - lo) + lo

`gamma < 1` expands the dynamic range of dark values (higher
contrast); `gamma > 1` compresses it.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gamma_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Intensity tensor. |
| `gamma` | `Float` | `...` | — | Exponent (scalar or broadcastable to `x`). Drawing it randomly is the caller's concern (a single `jax.random.uniform`). |
| `value_range` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | The `(lo, hi)` bracket to normalise within. `None` (default) uses the per-tensor `min` / `max`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gamma_contrast -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
